name: Shan Jiang
title: Research Fellow
bio: Shan Jiang is a Research Fellow at Deakin University and a PhD candidate at the Macquarie University Centre for the Health Economy (MUCHE). His research focuses on equity-informative economic evaluation — particularly distributional and dynamic distributional cost-effectiveness analysis (DCEA / D-DCEA) — methodological development in economic evaluation, advanced health economic modelling, and the economics of genomics and precision medicine.
researchStatement: My research focuses on equity-informative economic evaluation — particularly distributional and dynamic distributional cost-effectiveness analysis (DCEA / D-DCEA) — and advanced health economic modelling. I build decision tools that assess not only how much health a programme produces, but who benefits, who is missed, and how fairness evolves over time, and apply these methods across genomics, cancer screening, and global health.
